mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the ':' keyword in C++?
mcqs.explanation: The ':' keyword is used to implement inheritance in C++.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between public and private inheritance in C++?

options.text: Public inheritance allows access to private members
Private inheritance allows access to public members
Public inheritance allows access to public members
Private inheritance restricts access to private members

mcqs.explanation: Private inheritance restricts access to private members of the base class.

mcqs.question: Can a derived class inherit from multiple base classes in C++?

options.text: Only if the base classes have a common base class
Only if the derived class is an abstract class

mcqs.explanation: A derived class can inherit from multiple base classes in C++ using multiple inheritance.

mcqs.question: What is the diamond problem in C++?

options.text: A problem that arises when a derived class inherits from multiple base classes
A problem that arises when a derived class inherits from a single base class
A problem that arises when a base class has multiple derived classes
A problem that arises when a derived class has multiple base classes with a common base class

mcqs.explanation: The diamond problem is a problem that arises when a derived class inherits from multiple base classes that have a common base class, resulting in ambiguity.

mcqs.question: How is the diamond problem resolved in C++?
mcqs.explanation: The diamond problem is resolved using virtual inheritance, where the common base class is declared as a virtual base class.

mcqs.question: What is the role of the 'virtual' keyword in C++?
mcqs.explanation: The 'virtual' keyword is used to declare a virtual function, which can be overridden by a derived class.

mcqs.question: What is a pure virtual function in C++?

options.text: A function that must be implemented by a derived class
A function that can be implemented by a derived class
A function that is implemented by a base class
A function that is not implemented by a base class

mcqs.explanation: A pure virtual function is a virtual function that must be implemented by a derived class.

mcqs.question: What is an abstract class in C++?

options.text: A class that contains at least one pure virtual function
A class that contains only pure virtual functions
A class that contains no pure virtual functions

mcqs.explanation: An abstract class is a class that contains at least one pure virtual function and cannot be instantiated.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between an abstract class and an interface in C++?

options.text: An abstract class can contain both pure virtual and non-virtual functions
An interface can contain both pure virtual and non-virtual functions
An abstract class can only contain pure virtual functions
An interface can only contain non-virtual functions

mcqs.explanation: An abstract class can contain both pure virtual and non-virtual functions, while an interface can only contain pure virtual functions.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'override' keyword in C++?
mcqs.explanation: The 'override' keyword is used to override a virtual function in a derived class.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between static and dynamic binding in C++?
mcqs.explanation: Dynamic binding occurs at runtime, while static binding occurs at compile-time.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'final' keyword in C++?

options.text: To prevent a function from being overridden

mcqs.explanation: The 'final' keyword is used to prevent a function from being overridden in a derived class.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a 'const' and a 'constexpr' in C++?

options.text: A 'const' is used to declare a constant variable
A 'constexpr' is used to declare a constant variable
A 'const' is used to declare a variable that can be changed
A 'constexpr' is used to declare a variable that can be changed

mcqs.explanation: A 'constexpr' is used to declare a constant variable that can be evaluated at compile-time.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'decltype' keyword in C++?

options.text: To get the type of an expression

mcqs.explanation: The 'decltype' keyword is used to get the type of an expression.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'auto' keyword in C++?

options.text: To automatically deduce the type of a variable

mcqs.explanation: The 'auto' keyword is used to automatically deduce the type of a variable.
